content: {"perspective": "Economic", "answer": "The search results provide direct Prolon/L-Nutra branded FMD pricing plus several generic lifestyle intervention cost-effectiveness references. On Prolon cost: Sources 1, 3, 6, 9, and 10 all disclose the Prolon 5-Day Fasting-Mimicking Program retail prices: Source 3 lists the SKU ladder — Original $205 (discounted $185), Gen3 $205 ($185), Expanded Variety $215 ($195), Next Gen $255 ($215) per 5-day kit; Source 6 (Prolon's own marketing) says 'One round of the Prolon 5-Day FMD costs between $175 and $195'; Source 7 (Business Insider) reported Prolon meal kits 'costing between $250 and $300'; Source 9 (Healthline) states 'A 5-day ProLon fasting kit costs around $185, or $37 per day.' Converting to program cost: if the Burg-Peet protocol is a monthly 5-day FMD for one year, that is 12 cycles × ~$185 = ~$2,220 per patient per year in branded supplement cost alone, before any clinician time. This is substantial and very likely drives the majority of the non-cost-effectiveness verdict. On generic lifestyle intervention cost-effectiveness: Source 11 (PMC7518987) discusses the DPP (Diabetes Prevention Program) lifestyle change intervention in the context of 'Dominant: less costly and more beneficial' outcomes — showing that low-cost generic lifestyle interventions CAN achieve dominant status. Source 12 (PMC7472582, India lifestyle trial) is a trial-based CEA of a non-IF lifestyle intervention. Source 13 (ScienceDirect Glechner et al.) reports 'physical activity interventions appear cost saving and that diet and diet-with-physical-activity interventions are likely to be cost-effective.' Sources 14 and 15 (PMC7174913, Chinese prediabetic population) model lifestyle interventions with 3% absolute risk reduction in a resource-limited setting. Synthesis: the non-cost-effectiveness verdict of the Burg-Peet FMD study is almost certainly driven by the ~$2,200/year Prolon/L-Nutra supplement cost, NOT by the underlying fasting behavior. An unbranded physician-prescribed TRE or 5:2 protocol, which costs essentially zero beyond the patient's own food, would likely achieve dominant or near-dominant cost-effectiveness in the same model framework even with significantly smaller effect sizes. Information gap: no source directly models unbranded TRE/5:2 cost-effectiveness — this remains a notable evidence gap that Dr. Whitfield would flag as a high-priority question for future research.", "key_findings": ["Prolon 5-Day FMD kit retail price is approximately $175-$255 per 5-day kit (Sources 3, 6, 7, 9) — roughly $37/day.", "The Burg-Peet one-year monthly FMD protocol implies ~12 × $185 = $2,220 per patient per year in branded supplement cost before any clinician time, a substantial cost burden almost certainly driving the non-cost-effectiveness verdict.", "Generic lifestyle interventions (DPP, Indian trial, Chinese modeling) are typically cost-effective or even dominant (less costly and more beneficial) in high-risk populations (Sources 11-15).", "Physical activity interventions appear cost-saving; diet and diet+PA interventions are likely to be cost-effective (Source 13, Glechner et al. ScienceDirect).", "Unmet need: no source directly models an unbranded TRE or 5:2 cost-effectiveness, meaning the fasting behavior is bundled with commercial supplement cost in the only available IF economic evaluation. An unbranded protocol would likely achieve dominant status — but this is unproven and is a high-priority gap."], "sources_used": ["https://prolonlife.com/products/prolon-offer?srsltid=AfmBOoos1nV44lFPAO7su4Nbf9wd6c4TXlHo8DKotet4vBYNQnfzfHqs", "https://l-nutrahealth.com/products/prolon-offer?srsltid=AfmBOoquHXhARtLxIWEP3wNz5Fk3R_16YYIGu4jW-bU_R0qfIqhEBWPn", "https://prolonlife.com/products/prolon-offer-24?srsltid=AfmBOoqJRMYLX9V-AfBOwj-mfDyz5e95CvFxah34XYLFYhMT_94ZBeD1", "https://www.amazon.com/Fasting-Program-Developed-Ingredients-Extended/dp/B07KXZ9JX1", "https://www.thedailybeast.com/prolon-fast-review/", "https://prolonlife.com/blogs/stories/how-prolon-saves-you-money-time?srsltid=AfmBOoolQ3DS3yyUfRiXs-PK-bi5Vccrdoc_VlDFy_PF5I-cEOg1ebw1", "https://www.businessinsider.com/valter-longo-prolon-fasting-diet-cost-does-it-work-2018-4", "https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/prolon-diet", "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7518987/", "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7472582/", "https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1098301521016557", "https://www.researchgate.net/publication/340615558_The_Cost-Effectiveness_of_Lifestyle_Interventions_for_Preventing_Diabetes_in_a_Health_Resource-Limited_Setting", "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7174913/"]}
